terms.definition: The measurable degree to which elements of a socio-technical system act in mutually reinforcing alignment rather than in conflict.
The phenomenon whereby observing or exposing a system to new information alters that system's subsequent state.
The mean external force per agent that limits behavioral degrees-of-freedom (e.g., law, scarcity, community surveillance); denoted P.
A structural similarity that allows patterns in one domain to map onto another, enabling cross-domain imitation.
A non-linear qualitative change in a system’s macroscopic order parameters when a control variable crosses a critical threshold.
The presence of overlapping norms or constraints that allow a system to maintain order when one element fails.
The breadth of readily accessible pleasure options available to an agent; symbol H.
The rate at which disorder or information entropy enters a system from its environment; symbol E.
The count of socially permissible actions or partners available to an individual at a given time.
